meta_title: For Freelancers: passive income to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to passive income and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Passive Income to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop staring at a blank screen. Learn how to generate passive income streams by building micro-SaaS projects with this expert AI builder checklist.
content.answer_block: Stop chasing massive unicorns. Instead, identify a narrow, recurring pain point in your existing freelance workflow, build a micro-SaaS solution, and automate the delivery. By leveraging AI-assisted coding tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can ship high-value, low-maintenance assets that generate passive income while you sleep.
content.intro:
The freelance graveyard is littered with developers who spend months hunting for the 'perfect' startup idea, only to run out of runway before writing a single line of code. You’re trading time for money, stuck in a cycle of client deadlines, while your creative potential sits dormant, waiting for a spark that never comes.

But what if your next project wasn't a gamble? By shifting your focus from 'big ideas' to 'micro-automations,' you can turn your daily technical hurdles into scalable passive income streams. This isn't about building the next Facebook; it's about building the tools you already need, automating them, and letting the market do the rest.

steps.title: Identify a Micro-Pain Point
steps.description: Analyze your last 30 days of client work. Find a repetitive task you performed manually that could be solved by a focused script or API integration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my recent freelance project logs and identify three repetitive tasks that could be automated into a standalone micro-SaaS tool.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Identify 3 micro-SaaS opportunities based on my current project directory files'

steps.title: Architect the Minimum Viable Asset
steps.description: Define the smallest scope possible. If it takes more than 48 hours to build, it’s too big. Focus on a single input-to-output transformation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a technical architecture document for a micro-SaaS that performs [Task]. Keep it serverless and low-cost.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a project structure for a serverless micro-SaaS using Supabase and Next.js'

steps.title: Generate Boilerplate with Claude Code
steps.description: Use Claude Code to scaffold the entire application structure, ensuring type safety and clean, modular code from the very first commit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate the core business logic and database schema for the micro-SaaS architecture defined in step 2.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Initialize project with Next.js, Tailwind, and Supabase integration'

steps.title: Automate the Value Delivery
steps.description: Ensure the product delivers value without manual intervention. Use webhooks and background jobs to handle the heavy lifting.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a background worker script that processes user input and emails the result automatically.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a serverless function to handle automated recurring tasks via cron jobs'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Ship to Vercel or a similar platform. Focus on getting a live URL so you can begin validating with real users immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment checklist for a Vercel-hosted micro-SaaS, including environment variables and database migrations.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure project for production deployment and run initial build checks'

pro_tips: Always build for a niche you are currently working in; you already understand the customer's pain points.
Charge a one-time 'lifetime' fee for the first 10 users to validate demand before moving to a subscription model.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your core logic before you even write the UI; it prevents regression bugs.
Focus on 'boring' problems—invoicing, data formatting, or SEO tagging—these have the highest willingness-to-pay.
Don't build a custom auth system; integrate Clerk or Supabase Auth to save 20+ hours of development time.
content.next_steps: Once deployed, set up a simple landing page and share your tool in one niche community (Reddit, IndieHackers, or Twitter) to gather your first three pieces of feedback.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the strategy, and the blueprint to stop trading hours for dollars. Build a product that works for you, not the other way around. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
